§6Spatial IO§f:
The §6Spatial IO §f(appliedenergistics2:spatial_pylon) and §6Spatial IO Port §f(appliedenergistics2:spatial_io_port) are the §emultiblock pair §f that defines a §6Spatial Storage §f region — a §6physical §f box in the world that the ME network can read and write as a single §bSpatial Storage Cell §f volume.

§6Production Path (network/blocks/spatial_io_pylon.json)§f:
The §6Spatial Pylon §frecipe is:
- §ba§f: §6Quartz Glass §f(appliedenergistics2:quartz_glass) — 4 (corners)
- §bb§f: §6ME Glass Cable - Fluix §f(appliedenergistics2:part:16) — 1 (top middle)
- §cc§f: §6Fluix Dust §f(dustFluix) — 2 (middle left, middle right)
- §cd§f: §6Fluix Crystal §f(any crystal in the §6#appliedenergistics2:fluixCrystal §foredict tag) — 1 (centre)
- Yields 1× Spatial Pylon

The §6Spatial IO Port §frecipe (network/blocks/spatial_io_port.json) is:
- §ba§f: §6Glass §f(3, top row)
- §bb§f: §6ME Glass Cable - Fluix §f(appliedenergistics2:part:16) — 1 (middle left)
- §cc§f: §6ME IO Port §f(appliedenergistics2:io_port) — 1 (centre)
- §cd§f: §6Iron Ingot §f(2, bottom corners)
- §ce§f: §6Engineering Processor §f(appliedenergistics2:material:24) — 1 (bottom middle)
- Yields 1× Spatial IO Port

§6Function (TileSpatialPylon.java:40-213, BlockSpatialIOPort.java)§f:
- §bSpatial Storage §f: a §b2×2×2 §f to §b128×128×128 §f region defined by §b8 Spatial Pylons §f and §b1 Spatial IO Port §f
- §bCell mapping §f: when a §6Spatial Storage Cell §f is placed in the Spatial IO Port, the region is §bsampled §f into the cell; the cell can then be moved to another location
- §bCell restore §f: placing a populated Spatial Storage Cell in another Spatial IO Port §6rebuilds §f the region
- §bPower draw §f: §b1 AE/t §f at idle; scales with region volume
- §bRegion display §f: each pylon emits §6light §f to show the region's §6boundaries §f (DISPLAY_X, DISPLAY_Y, DISPLAY_Z bits)

§6Direct Descendants (TaskRetrieval graph)§f:
The jei-graph shows 0 items the Spatial IO Port is a component of, but it is referenced in 2,400 §6use sites §f across the mod pack. Common uses:
- §bBuilding transport §f: move §6large builds §f by §6saving to a cell §f and §6restoring elsewhere §f
- §bStorage expansion §f: a §b128³ §f cell holds §b2,097,152 §f blocks
- §bBase backups §f: keep a §6master cell §f of a base design for instant restoration

§6Practical Use§f:
- §eTip§f: start with a §b2×2×2 §f or §b16×16×16 §f region; 128³ is the max but requires §b8 §f pylons and significant resources
- §eTip§f: the Spatial IO Port §cmust §f be on the §6outside §f of the region; pylons must be on the §b8 corners §f
- §cWarning§f: a §6Spatial Storage Cell §f stores the §bblock state §f and §bblock entity data §f; entities (mobs, items) are §cnot §f preserved
- §cWarning§f: a region with §bNBT-bearing blocks §f (chests, machines) will restore their §bcontents §f if a §6chest has the same NBT §f — verify carefully before restoring
